title: Layer-wise Cross-Lingual Depression Detection from Speech: Analysis with Contrastive Alignment
innovation_summary: Layer-wise Cross-Lingual Depression Detection from Speech: Analysis with Contrastive Alignment: We propose CLeaD, a supervised contrastive alignment framework that maps WavLM embeddings from English and Mandarin into a shared clinical space, without parallel data or target-language.
why_it_matters: Overall signal 76/100 driven by novelty 100 and practical impact 76.
Primary categories: Mandarin, WavLM embeddings, cross-lingual generalization, leave-one-speaker-out evaluation, monolingual English, speaker identity leakage.
